methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: Manager calls calculateDepositShares() using TVL-based previewDeposit
Manager calls executeDeposit() minting shares + forwarding to connector
sinks: Base tokens forwarded to connector via addLiquidity
data_flows.trust_changes: Untrusted user input becomes queued request; manager controls calculation and execution timing
exposure_risks: TVL check runs AFTER transferFrom, so deposited tokens inflate TVL() making check stricter than intended
manipulation_risks: Share price manipulation between deposit and calculateDepositShares via TVL oracle manipulation
Manager timing of calculateDepositShares can be gamed to assign advantageous share prices

path: Manager calls executeWithdraw() burning shares + transferring base token
transformations: shares -> base token amount via previewRedeem
sinks: User receives base tokens minus withdrawal fee
data_flows.trust_changes: Queued request calculated by manager; fulfillment gated by asset availability
exposure_risks: fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup event emits 0 for totalCBAmount due to ordering bug
manipulation_risks: resetMiddle followed by re-calculation inflates totalCBAmount leading to excess token retrieval or incorrect pro-rata distribution

path: Connectors use valueOracle for price conversion
Result aggregated as totalTVL - totalDebt + base token balance - pending deposits
transformations: Connector-specific TVL -> base token value via oracle
sinks: Used in share price calculation (totalAssets), fee calculations, deposit limits
data_flows.trust_changes: External oracle prices trusted for valuation; connector implementations trusted for position TVL
validation_points: Oracle staleness checks in Chainlink connector
exposure_risks: MorphoBlueConnector ADDS borrow amount instead of subtracting, inflating TVL
UNIv3Connector uses aggregate pool position key, potentially overstating TVL
AerodromeConnector ignores staked LP balance in TVL
manipulation_risks: Oracle manipulation affects share price for all deposits/withdrawals
TVL inflation via Morpho borrow positions directly inflates share value

transformations: Calldata bytes encoded into EIP-712 hash (non-standard: bytes not pre-hashed)
sinks: Arbitrary function call on destination contract
data_flows.trust_changes: Multiple owner signatures required (threshold >= 2); executor must be msg.sender
exposure_risks: EIP-712 non-compliance: bytes data not pre-hashed per spec, may cause signing tool incompatibility
manipulation_risks: gasLimit parameter could be set too low causing silent failures at destination

path: Branch 1: AccountingManager calls -> Watcher verification -> transfer newAmount
Branch 2: Active connector or flashLoan calls -> direct transfer
Branch 3: Swap handler route -> route verification -> transfer
transformations: For AccountingManager branch: amount may be reduced to newAmount via Watcher
sinks: Tokens transferred to msg.sender (accounting manager, connector, or swap route)
data_flows.trust_changes: Flash loan contract has same trust level as active connectors
exposure_risks: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is empty - no actual verification of withdrawal amounts
Flash loan contract can drain tokens from any connector with no additional checks
manipulation_risks: Any entity with flashLoan role can extract tokens from connectors without watcher oversight

path: Position index lookup via isPositionUsed mapping
Add: push to holdingPositions array, set isPositionUsed
Remove: swap-and-pop pattern, update isPositionUsed for swapped element
sinks: holdingPositions array and isPositionUsed mapping in Registry
data_flows.trust_changes: Only enabled connectors can modify positions
exposure_risks: Swap-and-pop key recomputation uses calculatorConnector from HoldingPI instead of original msg.sender, causing key mismatch if they differ
manipulation_risks: Key mismatch during position removal corrupts isPositionUsed mapping, leaving stale entries and preventing correct lookups

core_rules: Deposits and withdrawals are queued and processed in batches by managers
Share price derived from TVL across all connectors plus idle base tokens
Waiting periods enforce delay between calculation and execution to allow price verification
Withdraw requests lock shares to prevent transfer during pending withdrawal
Fee system: withdrawal fee (instant), management fee (time-based), performance fee (profit-based with lock)
economic_incentives: Performance fee aligned with vault profit (with 12-48 hour lock window)
Management fee proportional to AUM minus fee receiver shares
checkIfTVLHasDroped allows anyone to reset performance fee if profit drops - protects depositors
governance_mechanisms: Timelock-protected maintainer role for configuration changes
Watcher role for monitoring and reverting suspicious actions
Emergency role for pause/unpause and rescue operations
Governor role for changing vault addresses

edge_cases.scenario: MorphoBlueConnector._getPositionTVL adds borrow amount instead of subtracting it
edge_cases.actual_behavior: TVL = supplyAmount + borrowAmount + collateralValue (borrow ADDED not subtracted)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker borrows maximum on Morpho to inflate vault TVL by 2x the borrow amount, inflating share price for existing holders or enabling performance fee extraction on phantom profits

edge_cases.scenario: Registry.onlyVaultMaintainer modifier uses OR instead of AND in condition
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Either vault maintainer OR emergency role can call protected functions
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Requires BOTH being vault maintainer AND having emergency role simultaneously due to (!=maintainer || !emergency) logic
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: addConnector, updateConnectorTrustedTokens, and removeTrustedPosition are effectively broken unless maintainer also holds EMERGENCY_ROLE in the global AccessControl

edge_cases.scenario: resetMiddle for withdraw queue does not reset currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmount
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Resetting middle should allow clean re-calculation of withdrawal amounts
edge_cases.actual_behavior: calculateWithdrawShares accumulates into totalCBAmount additively; after resetMiddle, re-processing same requests doubles totalCBAmount
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Inflated totalCBAmount causes neededAssetsForWithdraw to be doubled, potentially blocking fulfillment or causing incorrect pro-rata distribution in executeWithdraw

edge_cases.scenario: UNIv3Connector._getPositionTVL uses pool-level aggregate position instead of specific NFT tokenId
edge_cases.expected_behavior: TVL reflects only the liquidity of the specific NFT position owned by the connector
edge_cases.actual_behavior: pool.positions(key) returns aggregate liquidity for ALL positions at the same tick range owned by the NonfungiblePositionManager, potentially overstating TVL
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If other users have positions through the same NFT manager at identical tick ranges, the connector reports inflated TVL affecting share pricing

edge_cases.scenario: fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup emits event after zeroing totalCBAmount
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Event should log the actual totalCBAmount before zeroing
edge_cases.actual_behavior: totalCBAmount is set to 0 before emit, so event always logs 0
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct financial impact but breaks off-chain monitoring and auditing capabilities

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Watcher should verify that withdrawal amounts from connectors are legitimate
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Function body is empty, providing no verification at all
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious manager can extract arbitrary amounts from connectors to AccountingManager without any watcher-level validation

edge_cases.expected_behavior: currentFeeShares should not exceed totalSupply to avoid underflow
edge_cases.actual_behavior: preformanceFeeSharesWaitingForDistribution is not yet minted but is subtracted from totalSupply; if large enough, causes underflow revert
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A large pending performance fee can block management fee collection entirely, as totalShares - currentFeeShares would revert

edge_cases.scenario: UniswapValueOracle silently truncates amount to uint128
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Revert or handle amounts > uint128.max correctly
edge_cases.actual_behavior: uint128(amount) silently truncates, returning incorrect (much smaller) value
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If a position has extremely large token amounts, the oracle returns a truncated value, understating TVL

edge_cases.scenario: AerodromeConnector._getPositionTVL ignores staked LP in gauge
edge_cases.expected_behavior: TVL should include both unstaked and staked LP token balances
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only counts IERC20(pool).balanceOf(address(this)), missing gauge-staked liquidity
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: After staking LP in gauge, TVL drops to 0 for that position, understating vault TVL and share price

edge_cases.scenario: TVL() check in deposit() runs after token transfer but before totalAWFDeposit update
edge_cases.expected_behavior: TVL should not include pending deposit amounts
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Transferred tokens inflate balanceOf but totalAWFDeposit not yet updated, making TVL appear higher and potentially rejecting valid deposits
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct exploit but legitimate deposits may be rejected when vault is near capacity

edge_cases.scenario: Registry position removal swap-and-pop uses wrong key for isPositionUsed update
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Key for swapped element should use same computation as original insertion (msg.sender)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses calculatorConnector from HoldingPI struct, which may differ from the connector that created the position
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Corrupted isPositionUsed mapping causes positions to become unfindable or causes index collisions, breaking position management

edge_cases.scenario: Keepers EIP-712 encoding does not hash dynamic bytes type
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Per EIP-712 spec, dynamic types like bytes should be encoded as keccak256(value)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Raw bytes data is passed to abi.encode without pre-hashing
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Non-standard encoding may cause incompatibility with EIP-712 compliant signing tools; no direct security impact since signatures still work internally

edge_cases.scenario: Flash loan contract included in onlyManager modifier trust boundary
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Flash loan should have restricted permissions, not full manager access
edge_cases.actual_behavior: registry.flashLoan() address can call any onlyManager function on AccountingManager and any connector
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Compromised or malicious flash loan contract can execute arbitrary manager operations including calculating deposits, executing withdrawals, and triggering fee collection

edge_cases.scenario: ChainlinkOracleConnector does not normalize output to base token decimals
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Output should be in base token decimal precision for consistent TVL calculations
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Output precision depends on Chainlink feed decimals and asset decimals, not base token decimals
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: For token pairs with mismatched decimals (e.g., 18-decimal token priced via 8-decimal feed into 6-decimal base), values are off by orders of magnitude

edge_cases.scenario: executeDeposit reverts when no deposits are ready to process
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Gracefully handle case when no deposits meet the waiting time criterion
edge_cases.actual_behavior: processedBaseTokenAmount remains 0, connector check branch always reverts with NoyaAccounting_INVALID_CONNECTOR
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No financial impact; wastes gas for manager calls when no deposits are ready

assumption_violations: Registry onlyVaultMaintainer assumes OR logic but implements AND due to boolean operator error
MorphoBlue TVL assumes borrow is subtracted but code adds it
UniswapV3 TVL assumes pool.positions(key) returns only this connector's liquidity
Watchers verify function assumes future implementation will add checks, but currently provides zero protection
Management fee assumes totalSupply always >= currentFeeShares, but unminted performance fee shares can violate this
Position registry removal assumes calculatorConnector equals the connector that created the holdingPositionId key
game_theory_risks: checkIfTVLHasDroped is public: anyone can reset performance fee shares during temporary oracle/TVL dips, griefing strategy managers
Manager controls timing of calculateDepositShares and calculateWithdrawShares, can front-run known TVL changes
Inflating TVL via Morpho borrow positions allows extracting performance fees on non-existent profits
Withdrawal fee creates asymmetric cost that penalizes rapid repositioning but fee goes to a single receiver
integration_risks: Chainlink oracle staleness check (2 hours) may be too aggressive or lenient depending on the asset
Uniswap TWAP oracle period (1800s) may be insufficient for manipulation resistance on low-liquidity pairs
Pendle market oracle parameters (twapDuration=10) may be too short for reliable pricing
Curve calc_withdraw_one_coin may be manipulable via flash loans affecting virtual prices
Balancer pool TVL calculation assumes weighted pool type, incorrect for stable/composable pools
calculation_errors: MorphoBlueConnector._getPositionTVL: supplyAmount + borrowAmount + collateralValue should be supplyAmount - borrowAmount + collateralValue
BalancerConnector._getPositionTVL: potential overflow in 1e18 * token1bal * lpBalance for large pools
ChainlinkOracleConnector: missing feed decimals normalization causes result in feed-decimal precision instead of base-token precision
UniswapValueOracle: uint128 truncation of amount silently loses precision for values > 2^128

token_semantics.notes: Assumed to be a standard ERC20 like USDC/USDT; fee-on-transfer tokens would break deposit queue accounting since totalAWFDeposit tracks nominal amounts not received amounts
ERC4626 vault shares; transfers restricted by withdrawRequestsByAddress lock; standard ERC4626 deposit/mint/withdraw/redeem all revert
Reward token from Curve gauge harvesting

invariants.name: share_lock_conservation
invariants.rule: For all users: balanceOf(user) >= withdrawRequestsByAddress[user]; enforced in _update override

invariants.name: deposit_queue_ordering
invariants.rule: first <= middle <= last; first = executed, middle = calculated, last = pending

invariants.name: withdraw_queue_ordering
invariants.rule: first <= lastId <= middle <= last; withdrawal group bounds execution window

invariants.name: tvl_base_token_separation
invariants.rule: balanceOf(this) >= totalAWFDeposit at all times; idle TVL = balanceOf - totalAWFDeposit

invariants.name: holding_position_index_consistency
invariants.rule: VIOLATED: swap-and-pop during removal uses calculatorConnector instead of original connector for key recomputation

invariants.name: withdraw_group_totalCBAmount_accuracy
invariants.rule: VIOLATED: resetMiddle does not reset totalCBAmount, allowing double-counting on re-calculation
